title: Making Waves for Water Day
story: Every drop counts on World Water Day, celebrated each March 22! People around the globe organize beach cleanups, share water-saving tips on social media, and learn about communities that need clean drinking water. Sagendorf would go on to draw the famous spinach-eating sailor for decades, delighting fans with his artistic talent and humor.
activity_question: Where was Forest 'Bud' Sagendorf born?
bonus_fact: Popeye's famous catchphrase is 'I yam what I yam!'

title: Rock Star's Journey: From Netherlands to Australia
story: Guitar strings vibrated with talent when Harry Vanda was born in 1947. Starting life in Voorburg, Netherlands, he grew up to become a rock star. Vanda's musical journey led him to Australia, where he rocked as a guitarist, songwriter, and producer. On March 22, his birthday, fans celebrate the man behind the Easybeats' hit 'Friday On My Mind'.
activity_question: What famous song is Harry Vanda associated with?
follow_up_question: Can you name another job Harry Vanda had besides being a guitarist?

title: A Star Rises in Brooklyn
story: Lights, camera, action! In 1991, a future star was born in Brooklyn, New York. Dominique Fishback entered the world on March 22, destined for the spotlight. This talented actress would grow up to dazzle audiences with her performances, including her role in the buzzy TV series 'Swarm'. Fishback's journey from Brooklyn kid to screen sensation shows that big dreams can come true with hard work and passion.

title: A Pitching Star Is Born
story: Ramón Martínez threw his way into baseball history. Born in Santo Domingo on March 22, 1968, this Dominican pitcher would become a force on the mound. Martínez's blazing fastballs and clever changeups earned him a spot as an MLB All-Star in 1990. But his crowning achievement came in 1995 when he pitched a no-hitter for the Los Angeles Dodgers, leaving batters baffled and fans cheering.

title: Samba-Rock Star Born in Rio
story: Samba-rock legend Jorge Ben burst onto the scene in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Born on March 22, 1942, he would grow up to blend samba rhythms with rock and roll, creating a fresh sound. Ben's catchy tunes, like 'Mas que Nada', helped put Brazilian music on the world map. His tropical beats and clever lyrics made people dance from Rio's beaches to far-off lands.
activity_question: What type of music did Jorge Ben help create?
bonus_fact: Jorge Ben's real last name is Menezes, but he uses 'Ben' as his stage name.

title: Skating Stars Shine on Swiss Ice
story: Gliding gracefully across the rink, Soviet skaters Ekaterina Gordeeva and Sergei Grinkov dazzled the crowd in Geneva. Their flawless performance on March 22, 1986, earned them the Ice Pairs World Championship title. The talented duo's perfect synchronization and breathtaking lifts left spectators in awe. Their victory showcased the incredible skill and artistry of figure skating, inspiring young athletes around the world to chase their dreams on ice.
activity_question: Where did Gordeeva and Grinkov win their championship?

title: HBO's Big Boxing Bash Begins
story: Pow! Bam! In 1986, HBO threw the first punch in an epic boxing showdown. The TV network launched a tournament to crown one true heavyweight champion. On March 22, fighters from around the world stepped into the ring, ready to battle it out. Fans were glued to their screens, eager to see who would be the last boxer standing.

title: A Powerful Duet Hits the Charts
story: Voices soared as Patti LaBelle and Michael McDonald joined forces in 1986. Their soulful duet 'On My Own' captured hearts, climbing to the top of music charts. Released on March 22, the song's emotional lyrics and blend of their unique styles struck a chord with listeners everywhere. It became so popular that Billboard named it Song of the Year.
activity_question: What was the name of the hit song by Patti LaBelle and Michael McDonald?
bonus_fact: Patti LaBelle is known as the 'Godmother of Soul' for her powerful voice and long music career.
